scenarios.title: signs in with valid credentials @positive
scenarios.userAction: Fill the Acme Shop login form with valid credentials and click Sign in, expecting a dashboard greeting personalised for the user.
expectedAssertions: dashboard greeting is visible after submit

scenarios.title: rejects an invalid password @negative
scenarios.userAction: Fill the login form with a valid email and a wrong password, click Sign in, and expect the error banner to surface without leaving /login.
expectedAssertions: URL still matches /\/login/ after submit (no redirect)

locatorTable.notes: Assumes input has an associated <label>. Fall back to getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Email' }).
Submit button — role-based avoids matching page heading.
Greeting MAY be a heading; if it's a div with no role, keep CSS class and add WHY-comment. See pin 1.
Login error banners typically use role="alert". Fall back to getByTestId('login-error') if not. See pin 2.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved: greeting element type unknown — assumed heading role.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask FE team for heading semantics OR request data-testid="dashboard-greeting".

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4: alert role not confirmed — assumed role="alert" on the banner.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask team to add role="alert" or switch to getByTestId('login-error').

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q5: 7s defensive over-wait assumed — actual backend latency unverified.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If backend genuinely needs >5s (rate-limit cooldown), Stage 2 must raise per-assertion timeout to 10s rather than removing the wait silently.
